title: Darken continents to near-black and sharpen background

requirements.summary: Change continent fill/outline color from blue to near-black in AnimatedWorldMap.tsx to match the Black, Gold & White theme
acceptanceCriteria: Continents on the animated world map render in a near-black color, not blue
Continent color is visually consistent with the Black, Gold & White theme

file_operations.description: Update the continent stroke color from the current blue (#0ea5e9) to near-black (#0a0a0a or #111111). Locate the stroke style assignment for continent paths in the canvas rendering loop and replace the color value with a near-black hex value that matches the app's Black, Gold & White theme.

requirements.summary: Reduce pixelation and blurriness on the hero background to ensure sharp, crisp rendering across viewport sizes
acceptanceCriteria: The hero section background is visually sharp with no noticeable pixelation or blur
Background does not appear stretched or scaled in a lossy way
Sharpness is consistent across common viewport sizes

file_operations.description: Improve background image sharpness in the hero section by setting image-rendering CSS property to 'crisp-edges' or 'pixelated', ensuring the background image uses the highest-resolution asset available (frontend/public/assets/generated/world-map-bg-4k.dim_3840x2160.png), and applying object-fit and object-position properties to prevent lossy scaling. Verify that no CSS transform or filter properties introduce blur artifacts.

Ensure the canvas renders at native resolution without pixelation by setting canvas width/height attributes to match the CSS display size multiplied by devicePixelRatio, then scaling the canvas context accordingly. This prevents the canvas overlay from contributing to perceived blur or pixelation on high-DPI displays.

Add a global CSS rule for image-rendering to prevent browser interpolation on background images. Apply 'image-rendering: crisp-edges' or 'image-rendering: -webkit-optimize-contrast' to relevant selectors to ensure sharp rendering across browsers. Ensure this does not conflict with other image assets that need smooth rendering.
